title: Iran security chief Ali Larijani likely to be next supreme leader | The World | ABC NEWS
description: In the aftermath of the death of Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, attention has turned to who will succeed him. Ali Larijani, secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council — tasked with a wide remit in Iran's constitution to preserve the Islamic Revolution, territorial integrity and national sovereignty — has emerged as a frontrunner to lead the Islamic Republic. Iran-born journalist and Middle East expert, Tara Kangarlou spoke to The World's Girish Sawlani about what lies ahead for the future of the Iranian regime. title: Can Iran's Revolutionary Guards withstand US-Israeli strikes? | The World | ABC NEWS
description: As Israel and the United States continue striking targets within Iranian territory, the pillar of Iran's military, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, has warned it's going to unleash its greatest offensive operation. US President Donald Trump has called on IRGC troops to lay down their arms or face certain death. Middle East expert Adel Abdel Ghafar spoke to The World's Girish Sawlani about what lies ahead for the Iran's military and how much support can it get from militia groups, including Lebanon's Hezbollah and Yemen's Houthis. title: Gulf states flag response to Iran's 'heinous attacks' | The World | ABC NEWS
description: Iran has fired missiles at targets in nine countries in the region. They include the states that comprise the Gulf Cooperation Council - the UAE, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, Oman, Qatar and Kuwait, who have hinted they could launch their own strikes on Iran. They've issued a statement vowing to defend themselves against Iranian attacks "including the option of responding to the aggression". The World's Girish Sawlani spoke to Professor Amnon Aran, middle east expert from the University of London about the implications of a broader regional conflict. title: Critical oil route throttled by Iran conflict | The World | ABC NEWS
description: The war in the Middle East has caused global oil prices to surge. Iran has one of the world's largest oil reserves and has the power to effectively shut the Strait of Hormuz, the world's most critical oil trade route. Brent crude jumped 10% when energy markets opened for the week, but later settled to sit at just below 80 USD a barrel. Jorge León from Rystad Energy spoke to The World's Girish Sawlani about the impact of these developments on the energy industry. title: War in Middle East: US jets downed in 'friendly fire incident' in Kuwait | ABC NEWS
description: A fourth US service member has died during a US-Israel operation against Iran, while three US fighter jets were shot down in a friendly fire incident over Kuwait.  The six crewmembers of the US warplanes were able to eject to safety. US Defense Secretary has denied fears of Operation Epic Fury extending into a ‘forever war’ and ruled out claims of nation building.  US President Donald Trump had previously flagged the possibility of more American lives lost as the White House ramps up attacks on Iran. title: Trump says he will not get 'bored' of striking Iran | ABC NEWS
description: US President Donald Trump has warned a ‘big wave’ of attacks on Iran is coming as he explained the timeline of a US-Israel operation in the Middle East.  The US leader says Operation Epic Fury was his country’s ‘last, best chance’ to take out Tehran’s nuclear and missile threat but stopped short of including regime change in his explanation of the bombing campaign’s rationale.  Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth has backed the president’s rhetoric and rejected fears of an ‘endless war’. title: Israel, Hezbollah trade fire as Lebanese PM slams the Iran-backed militant group | ABC NEWS
description: Huge explosions have rocked the Lebanese capital of Beirut as Israel and Hezbollah exchange fire after the killing of Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.
